calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-25-2012 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-03-2012 Set For Trial On Nov-13-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Renewal Of Default, Original Entry Of Default And Original Default
Off Calendar May-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STEVEN KARO DBA PROPERTY MAINTENANCE SERVICE'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE RENEWAL OF DEFAULT, ORIGINAL ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND ORIGINAL DEFAULT JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Andrew Foster Shi of the Matlock Law Group (925.944.7131) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. Plaintiff's service by publication, even though permitted by the court on an unopposed application, requires that the default and default judgment be set aside because at the time that service by publication was made plaintiff knew defendant's residence address yet neither served defendant by mail as required by section 415.30 nor, once service by publication was accomplished, served a copy of the publication order by mail as required by section 415.50(b) and the order providing for service by publication. (Code of Civil Procedure 473(d) (void judgment); Transamerica Title Insurance Company v. Hendrix, (1995) 34 Cal. App. 4th 740 (reversing trial court's denial of motion to set aside under similar circumstances).) Defendant has shown he has a meritorious case, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action, and acted diligently to set aside the default judgment once discovered. (Rappleyea v. Campbell, (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975.) Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec. And Pos
Off Calendar Apr-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MOLALLA HOLDINGS, INC'S Motion To Compel Answers To Post-Judgment Discovery; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions.
Appearances: Anne-Leith Matlock of the Matlock Law Group, PC (925.944.7131) for defendant and responding party Steven Karo; no other appearances.
There being no other appearances, plaintiff's counsel is deemed to have stipulated to the matter being decided by a Judge Pro Tem. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to Compel Answers to Post-Judgment Discovery GRANTED in part. Parties will meet and confer on date certain to serve verified responses. No sanctions. Court will prepare and file an order. (302/JPT) Pro Tem Judge: Rebecca Woodson; Not reported
